message:
feat(cli): add `cass resume` subcommand — resolve a session path into a ready-to-run harness resume command

New `cass resume` subcommand that takes a session file path (as
printed by `cass search` or `cass sessions`) and resolves it
into the exact harness-specific command needed to continue that
session inside its original agent.  Covers the five harnesses
cass already knows how to index: Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode,
pi_agent, and Gemini.

src/lib.rs

- New Commands::Resume variant with the following fields:
    path:  PathBuf                       — session file path
    agent: Option<String>                — override the detected
        harness name (accepted: claude, codex, opencode,
        pi_agent, gemini)
    exec:  bool (conflicts with shell/json) — replace the
        current process with the resolved command via exec()
    shell: bool (conflicts with json)    — emit a single
        shell-escaped command line on stdout suitable for
        `eval "$(cass resume ...)"`
    json:  bool (visible_alias "robot")  — emit a structured
        JSON payload for automation
  Default (no flags) prints the argv tokens one per line.

- Doc comment on the variant gives the three usage patterns:
    eval "$(cass resume /path/to/session.jsonl --shell)"
    $(cass resume /path/to/session.jsonl --shell) # direct exec
    cass resume /path/to/session.jsonl --exec

- New resume execution handler in execute_cli that:
    1. Normalizes the input path and reads the session
       metadata (program + agent_id + opencode DB schema
       version if applicable).
    2. Detects the harness from the file signature, overridden
       by --agent if supplied.
    3. For each harness, builds the native resume argv:
         claude:    claude --resume <session_id>
         codex:     codex resume --session-id <session_id>
         opencode:  opencode resume --db <db_path> --session <id>
         pi_agent:  pi-agent resume --session <session_file>
         gemini:    gemini --resume-transcript <transcript_path>
    4. Emits the result per the flag combination.

- describe_command() + is_robot_mode() updated to recognize
  the Resume variant so robot-mode log lines include a
  descriptive command string.

- New unit tests under cli_read_db_tests::resume_command_tests:
    resume_resolves_claude_session_argv
    resume_resolves_codex_session_argv
    resume_resolves_opencode_sqlite_session_argv
    resume_resolves_pi_agent_session_argv
    resume_resolves_gemini_transcript_argv
    resume_agent_override_overrides_detection
    resume_unknown_harness_returns_clean_error
    resume_shell_mode_shell_escapes_spaces
    resume_json_mode_emits_structured_payload
    resume_exec_mode_conflicts_with_shell_and_json

  Each test writes a minimal fake session file for the
  harness, calls the resume resolver, and asserts the argv
  matches the expected shape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
